case_title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A. VS. MICHAEL LY

MEMO IN OPPOS. TO MTN FOR ORDER VACATING AND SETTING ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT; DEC. OF CHARLES GLAUBERMAN FILED BY PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A.
REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF DEFS MTN TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT LY, MICHAEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MICHAEL LY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED A SUFFICIENT DECLARATION BY HIS ATTORNEY STATING THAT AN ANSWER WAS NOT TIMELY FILED DUE TO THE ATTORNEY'S MISTAKE. RELIEF IS MANDATORY UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES. IN ANY EVENT, RELIEF WAS PROMPTLY SOUGHT AND DISCRETIONARY RELIEF WOULD BE APPROPRIATE. DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY IS ORDERED TO PAY $202.50 FOR PLAINTIFF'S FEES AND COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH OBTAINING THE DEFAULT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-03-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT
GENERAL DENIAL FILED BY DEFENDANT LY, MICHAEL
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MADISON N. NGUYEN, ESQ., 408.643.0641, FOR DEFENDANT MICHAEL LY; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MICHAEL LY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS Granted. Defendant has provided a sufficient declaration by his attorney stating that an answer was not timely filed due to the attorney's mistake. Relief is mandatory under these circumstances. In any event, relief was promptly sought and discretionary relief would be appropriate. Defendant's attorney is ordered to pay $202.50 for Plaintiff's fees and costs associated with obtaining the default. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
